claim: An AI agent without memory is just autocomplete with ambition, leading to repeated explanations and out-of-context information.
actionable: Implement persistent memory systems for agents rather than relying on single-session context windows.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Eli Schilling: Hands On Agent Context & Memory Engineering with Oracle AI Database

claim: Bloated context windows lead to inconsistent responses, increased hallucination, and repeated mistakes in agent behavior.
actionable: Monitor context window size and implement summarization thresholds (e.g., at 80% capacity) to compact short-term memory into long-term summaries.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Eli Schilling: Hands On Agent Context & Memory Engineering with Oracle AI Database

claim: Memory architectures should include seven types: conversational, knowledge base, workflow, toolbox, entity, summary, and tool logs to support enterprise-scale agent operations.
actionable: Design separate database tables for each memory type and implement a memory manager that selectively populates prompts with relevant memory entries by ID rather than full text.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Eli Schilling: Hands On Agent Context & Memory Engineering with Oracle AI Database

claim: Use a unified database platform (e.g., Oracle) to store all data modalities (relational, vector, JSON, spatial, time-series) rather than multiple databases, reducing operational overhead.
actionable: Replace separate vector, relational, and graph databases with a converged database supporting on-the-fly vector embeddings and multi-modal queries.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Eli Schilling: Hands On Agent Context & Memory Engineering with Oracle AI Database

claim: Separate programmatic reads (conversation, knowledge base, workflow, entity, summary, toolbox) from agent-triggered operations to implement guardrails and prevent agents from autonomously accessing sensitive data.
actionable: Design memory manager to populate prompts programmatically, then allow agents to trigger only pre-defined tool calls and external retrievals rather than reading arbitrary memory.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Eli Schilling: Hands On Agent Context & Memory Engineering with Oracle AI Database

claim: Summarization at token thresholds (e.g., 80%) reduces context bloat from 80,000 tokens down to manageable levels while preserving long-horizon task capability.
actionable: Calculate context window utilization; when exceeding 80%, summarize conversation, store summary with ID, and continue agent loop with reduced token cost.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Eli Schilling: Hands On Agent Context & Memory Engineering with Oracle AI Database

claim: Reference memory entries by ID rather than embedding full text in prompts to reduce token utilization without losing context.
actionable: Store memory in database with unique IDs and pass only memory ID references in prompts; agent retrieves full content on-demand.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Eli Schilling: Hands On Agent Context & Memory Engineering with Oracle AI Database

claim: Agents with memory maintain flat token utilization across iterations while naive agents without memory experience exponential token growth with each new conversation turn.
actionable: Implement memory-backed agents and track token consumption charts over multiple conversation cycles to verify flat-line performance vs. naive baseline.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Eli Schilling: Hands On Agent Context & Memory Engineering with Oracle AI Database

claim: Context engineering, memory engineering, and harness engineering form an interconnected flywheel that drives agent learning and adaptation over time.
actionable: Design three-layer system: context assembly (pull relevant memory), agent loop (invoke LLM and act), and outer harness (extract and store memory units for next iteration).
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Eli Schilling: Hands On Agent Context & Memory Engineering with Oracle AI Database

claim: Just-in-time memory retrieval allows agents to fetch specific long-term memory during operations without bloating the initial context window.
actionable: Enable agents to query memory tables mid-execution when they encounter tasks requiring information not in current context.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Eli Schilling: Hands On Agent Context & Memory Engineering with Oracle AI Database
